persons.arguments_against: Two objections worth testing. (1) The biography says colonial secretary 'of Barbados' while the stint's 1878-1883 Colonial Secretary records sit under 'Windward Islands' — could these be two different W. Hely-Hutchinsons in neighbouring administrations? No: in this period Barbados was the seat of the Windward Islands government (the same governor administered both), so the Barbados colonial secretary naturally appears in the Windward Islands staff lists; the date (appointed Nov. 1877, first listed 1878) and the 'Hon. W. F.' initials match the biography exactly. (2) A same-name relative could be the 1890 Governor. No: the biography itself records 'gov. of Windward Is., 1889' and K.C.M.G. (1888), and the 1890 record reads 'Sir Walter Hely-Hutchinson ... K.C.M.G.' — the unique gubernatorial office, the knighthood timing, and the forename all agree. The chain's 1883-1890 listing gap corresponds precisely to his Malta chief-secretaryship (1883-89), which is career-shape corroboration rather than a discrepancy. The position_sim FAIL is purely the abbreviation 'gov.' scoring 11 against the long printed title.

evidence_cited.fact: Governor and Commander-in-Chief of the Windward Islands

persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second W. Hely-Hutchinson serving as colonial secretary in the Barbados/Windward Islands administration in 1878-83, or that the 1890 Governor record belongs to a different knighted Walter Hely-Hutchinson.

notes: Rare double-barrelled surname (6 officials corpus-wide), W. F. initials, two distinct offices agreeing with the biography at both ends of the chain, and a listing gap that exactly matches the Malta posting. The 1883 bio version's 'C.M.G. (1885)' is an OCR slip for 1883.
